title: Weather service warns of gale force winds in the Cape
description: GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - The South...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - The South African Weather Service (SAWS) has issued a yellow level 1 warning for damaging coastal wind and waves between Cape Point and Cape Agulhas on Tuesday.

Here is what you can expect on Tuesday 18 June:


> Western Cape:


Cloudy and cool with light rain in the west and south-west in the morning as well as in the late afternoon, otherwise partly cloudy. It will be windy over the interior at times.

The wind along the coast will be moderate to fresh north-westerly to westerly, but strong to gale force along the south-west coast. The expected UVB sunburn index: Low.


> Western half of the Eastern Cape:


Partly cloudy and cool, becoming fine over the interior from the afternoon. The wind along the coast will be moderate to fresh north-westerly, becoming south-westerly by the afternoon.


> Eastern half of the Eastern Cape:


Cloudy in the north in the morning, otherwise fine and cool but windy in places. The wind along the coast will be moderate to fresh south-westerly, becoming light north-westerly by the evening.


> Weather details for Tuesday:


TownMinimumMaximumWindForecastGeorge13°C22°CSW 0 km/hClear skies - partly cloudyKnysna14°C23°CSW 0 km/hClear skies - partly cloudyMossel Bay12°C22°CW 15 km/h Mostly sunny and nice Riversdale11°C23°CWNW 9 km/hPartly cloudy - cloudyOudtshoorn8°C23°CWNW 9 km/hPartly cloudy - clear skiesGraaff-Reinet6°C22°CNW 15 km/hSunshine - Patchy clouds

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
